purpose: fixed multi-file evaluation set for PDG build-scope and propagation work

defaults.note: keep engine differences out of this set; G4-8 handles engine baselines

cases.focus: cross-file callsite use -> callee summary -> assigned def bridge

initial_source.src/callee.rs: pub fn callee(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

cases.current_gap: current PDG build scope is likely to include the changed caller file but not the callee file, so cross-file summary-connected bridge edges are weak or absent

want_after_g4: propagation lane shows cross-file bridge from callsite use(x) into callee-side flow and back to assigned def(y)
callee-file local nodes or witness edges become visible as the reason for enrichment
avoid: broad caller-file fan-out to unrelated defs

cases.focus: cross-file wrapper temp variable plus return-flow into caller result

initial_source.src/core.rs: pub fn core(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

cases.current_gap: call graph should still show caller -> wrap -> core, but wrapper-file temp-flow and returned-result stitching are weak when only the changed file gets local DFG

want_after_g4: propagation lane retains temp alias flow around adapter::wrap
impacted witnesses include local_dfg or symbolic_propagation, not just plain call edges

cases.focus: imported callee result stitched into caller-side alias chain

initial_source.src/value.rs: pub fn make(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

repro.optional_extension: add `let alias2 = alias;` to force a 2-hop alias chain if the 1-hop shape is too small

cases.current_gap: caller-side alias edges can exist, but the imported callee result often fails to stitch into that chain when the callee file is outside local DFG scope

want_after_g4: result of value::make(x) flows into y -> alias -> out without falling back to call-only explanation
avoid: stale or broad alias edges that are not justified by the local chain

cases.focus: require-relative split fixture for temp alias and return-flow chain

repro.builder: split analyzer_hard_cases_callees_chain_alias_return.rb so the return-ish expression lives in leaf.rb and the temp alias lives in mid.rb

cases.current_gap: callers chain can remain visible through normal call graph, but multi-file temp alias / return-flow explanation is thin when DFG stays local to the changed file

want_after_g4: mid-file temp alias and leaf-file return-ish flow appear in propagation evidence
witnesses explain where the local data bridge entered the callers chain
avoid: collapse of the chain around temp alias nodes
fan-out outside the intended linear chain

cases.focus: cross-file dynamic send/public_send separation guard under broader PDG scope

repro.builder: split analyzer_hard_cases_dynamic_send_public_send.rb so target definitions live in targets.rb and dispatch calls live in router.rb

cases.current_gap: today this case is mainly a forward guard: scope expansion should not turn dynamic target separation into broad cross-file smearing

want_after_g4: local-var mediated target edges stay specific
avoid: all dynamic targets collapsing into one broad impacted set
